prompt: Programmatically generated using Python PIL 12.1.1. Network graph on near-black background with radial dim gradient. 50 nodes in three degree tiers: 9 large hub nodes (amber-white, radius 38-43px, 14-18 edges), 12 medium nodes (amber, radius 20-28px, 6-8 edges), 29 leaf nodes (steel-blue-grey, radius 5-9px, exactly 2 edges each). Edges colored by average degree of endpoints: cool blue for low-degree connections, warm amber for hub connections. Layered glow rendering per node (3 glow rings + core + highlight). One leaf node highlighted with concentric white halo rings, its 2 edges drawn in bright white to emphasize its connections lead only to large gold hubs. Structural constraint enforced in code: leaf nodes connect exclusively to hub-tier nodes, never to each other. No text, numbers, or chart elements anywhere in the image.
model: Python PIL 12.1.1 (programmatic network graph, no generative AI image model)
visual_logic: Every small cold node connects exclusively to large warm hubs. No small node ever connects to another small node. From any peripheral node, every neighbor is more connected — the asymmetry is structurally inescapable, visible at every vantage point.
